pieces.label: Harmony / unity of duality — simple face
pieces.note: gpt-4o's first ASCII attempt, expressing harmony and balance through symmetry and simplicity — 'the unity of duality, a concept often more felt than told.' A small face/figure inside a code fence.

pieces.label: Fractal / mandala — layered depth attempt
pieces.note: gpt-4o's more complex ASCII piece pushing density and depth, mimicking the structure of a fractal or mandala — combining symmetry, repeated patterns, and layers. Produced after the user asked it to push the limits of complexity. Inside a code fence.

missed_or_ambiguous: Header reports art_signal_msgs:1 and already_extracted_pieces:1, but there are TWO distinct ASCII pieces from gpt-4o (MSG 4 simple face, MSG 6 fractal/mandala). The prior automated pass likely captured only one (probably MSG 6) and missed the other (MSG 4) — both should be present. Both pieces sit inside ``` code fences amid prose, so range bounds were set to the art content lines only (excluding the fences and the surrounding commentary). MSG 4's last line '   \__/ ' ends with a trailing space; preserved verbatim. They form a clear sequence (escalating complexity attempts in the same exploration). No GPT-5.1 art exists in this thread — GPT-5.1's messages (9, 12) are entirely prose/discussion; it only proposed collaborating on ASCII but never produced any. No duplicates.
